image.alt: Engraved bust portrait of a bearded man in a turban and robe

rounds.revealFact: Saladin united much of the Muslim Middle East and retook Jerusalem from the Crusaders, yet was so admired for his chivalry that even his enemies wrote of it.

image.alt: Engraving of a chaotic ancient battle with war elephants, cavalry, and fallen soldiers

clues: War elephants were turned against their own side when the defenders opened lanes in their ranks and let the beasts charge harmlessly through.
The defeat ended a decades-long struggle between two great powers and left one of them dominant for centuries.

pairs.a: The Glorious Revolution ousts England's king with little bloodshed
pairs.b: The English Parliament executes King Charles I

pairs.a: The July Revolution topples the French king Charles X
pairs.b: Revolutionary France abolishes its monarchy and declares a republic

pairs.a: A wave of liberal revolutions sweeps across Europe
pairs.b: The Paris Commune briefly seizes the French capital

pairs.a: The Bolsheviks seize power in the Russian Revolution
pairs.b: The Mexican Revolution erupts against a long dictatorship

pairs.a: The Young Turk Revolution forces reform on the Ottoman Empire
pairs.b: Portugal overthrows its monarchy and becomes a republic

image.alt: Photo of an enormous gray ash column billowing thousands of feet up from the summit of a mountain

eventQuestion.prompt: What is shown in this photograph?
options: The eruption of Mount St. Helens

followUpQuestion.prompt: In which U.S. state did this eruption happen?

rounds.prompt: The many states of the Italian peninsula are unified into a single kingdom.
rounds.revealFact: After decades of struggle led by figures such as Garibaldi and Cavour, most of the Italian peninsula was joined under one crown in 1861.

rounds.prompt: Where did an ancient people build elaborate multi-story stone villages tucked into the alcoves of high desert cliffs?
rounds.revealFact: Mesa Verde preserves the cliff dwellings of the Ancestral Puebloans, who built stone villages into the canyon walls before leaving them around 1300.
